pattern: Each position's total rotation is the sum of all shifts at or after it, so a single suffix sum taken modulo twenty-six gives every letter's final offset in linear time.
hints: The letter at position i is rotated by every shift whose index is i or larger, so think about cumulative sums from the right.
Compute the running suffix total of shifts as you move from the last position toward the first.
Reduce each suffix total modulo twenty-six and rotate the corresponding letter by that amount, wrapping past 'z' back to 'a'.

edge_inputs.description: three letters with growing cumulative shifts
shift wraps z back around the alphabet
zero shifts leave the string unchanged
huge shift values exercise the modulo
exact multiple of 26 is a no-op rotation

stress.description: random lowercase strings with random large shifts of matching length
stress.generator_python:
import string
def gen(rng, size):
    n = rng.randint(1, max(1, size))
    s = ''.join(rng.choice(string.ascii_lowercase) for _ in range(n))
    shifts = [rng.randint(0, 1000000000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [s, shifts]
stress.note: large shift magnitudes stress the modular reduction
